content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Weekly Report (Mar 29 - Apr 4, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
This week, the ElizaOS registry saw a surge in plugin proposals focused on expanding the framework's utility across DeFi, social platforms, and governance. A major narrative emerged around **Autonomous Agent Spend Governance**, with intensive technical discussions on establishing secure, policy-driven payment layers for x402 executions. The community also made significant strides in agent identity and trust layers, validating the project's core philosophy of modularity and decentralized intelligence through integrations with protocols like MAXIA and MoltBridge.

## 🛠️ Key Developments
The registry received several new plugin submissions aimed at enhancing agent capabilities in financial analysis, social interaction, and permission management.

### New Plugin Integrations
*   **DeFi & Analytics:** Proposals were submitted for `@axnetfun/plugin-axnet` [#324](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/324), `@wzrd_sol/eliza-plugin` for AI model velocity signals [#325](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/325), and `@elizaos/plugin-nulucre` for wallet reputation scoring and TVL verification [#326](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/326).
*   **Social & Infrastructure:** New PRs were opened to add `plugin-roles` and `plugin-wechat` from the milady-ai ecosystem [#328](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/328), as well as `plugin-vybes` for vybes.fun integration [#327](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/327).
*   **Marketplace Readiness:** The MAXIA AI Marketplace plugin was reported as ready for submission, featuring five actions and a strict TypeScript implementation designed for secure agent-to-agent transactions [#6700](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6700).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
The week was dominated by deep architectural discussions regarding how agents handle financial autonomy and cryptographic identity.

### Active Discussions & Future Work
*   **Agent Spend Governance (Dreamline x402):** Extensive debate continued on [#6695](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6695) regarding the "Policy Facilitator" pattern. Key technical considerations include:
    *   Implementing **fail-closed defaults** to block payments if policy services are unreachable.
    *   Developing a **three-object contract** (`payment_required`, `payment_approval`, `payment_receipt`) to allow human-in-the-loop oversight for high-value transactions.
    *   Exploring the integration of MAXIA’s AIP Protocol for signed intent validation.
*   **Identity and Trust Layers:** Discussions on AgentID [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6688) and MoltBridge [#6501](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6501) focused on bridging the "trust gap" in agent marketplaces. Contributors are converging on W3C DIDs and on-chain spend caps (L0-L4 trust levels) as the standard for verifiable agent identity.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
Collaboration this week was characterized by high-level technical synthesis between different plugin developers. There is a clear trend of cross-pollination between projects; for instance, the x402 governance discussion is drawing on diagnostic RFCs and MAXIA’s on-chain escrow logic. The involvement of contributors like `majorelalexis-stack` and `JKHeadley` indicates a strong push toward aligning ElizaOS with broader industry standards like W3C DIDs and established A2A (agent-to-agent) registries. The community is actively questioning the core ElizaOS team on low-level event surfacing (e.g., x402 pre-execution signals), showing a transition from simple plugin additions to deep framework refinement.
